sourceMovie.title: A Haunting in Venice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Haunting in Venice (2023) stuck because it commits to its mystery premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kenneth Branagh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The mystery and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Pale Blue Eye
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Haunting in Venice clicked for you, The Pale Blue Eye (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Haunting in Venice?
faq.answer: Try The Pale Blue Eye, The Maltese Falcon, Crooked House—each shares DNA with A Haunting in Venice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Haunting in Venice?
faq.answer: A Haunting in Venice (2023) is best described as Mystery, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Haunting in Venice part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hercule Poirot (Kenneth Branagh) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
